message:
feat(triggers): implement per-row OLD/NEW pseudo-table values for DML triggers

Replace placeholder None values in trigger firing with actual row data,
enabling BEFORE/AFTER triggers to access OLD and NEW pseudo-tables:

Trigger row collection:
- Add collect_insert_trigger_rows() that evaluates INSERT source
  expressions (VALUES, SELECT, DEFAULT VALUES) into concrete row vectors
  mapped to table column layout
- Add collect_update_trigger_rows() that fetches matched rows, evaluates
  SET assignments against the old values to produce (old, new) pairs,
  handling both single-column and column-list assignments with row-value
  expressions
- Add collect_delete_trigger_rows() that fetches matched rows for OLD
  pseudo-table access
- Add helper methods: evaluate_insert_source_row(),
  map_insert_source_row_to_table_row(), find_column_index_case_insensitive()

Trigger dispatch integration:
- Add has_matching_triggers() predicate to skip row collection when no
  triggers match, avoiding unnecessary SELECT overhead
- Wire collected row data into fire_before_triggers() and
  fire_after_triggers() calls for INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE statements
- Fire triggers per-row (iterating over collected rows) rather than
  once-per-statement, matching SQLite's row-level trigger semantics
- When triggers are present, use collected row count for affected-rows
  instead of a separate count_matching_rows() call

Internal refactor:
- Extract select_matching_rows() from count_matching_rows() to share
  row-fetching logic between trigger collection and counting

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
